description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who has just performed two test restores (a daily and a weekly set).  
Your team lead wants three things, all to be created inside **/home/user/backup_testing**:

1. A tab-separated “side-by-side” comparison file that puts the three most important columns from the two restore manifests next to each other.  
2. A tiny human-readable summary that states how many data rows each manifest contains (headers must not be counted).  
3. A one-line-per-command log of every shell command you actually typed while producing items 1 & 2.

The directory **/home/user/backup_testing/manifests** already contains two comma-separated CSV files:

• /home/user/backup_testing/manifests/daily_restore_2023-01-15.csv  
• /home/user/backup_testing/manifests/weekly_restore_2023-01-15.csv  

Both files have exactly this header line (six columns, comma-separated):

ID,FILE_PATH,ORIGINAL_SIZE,RESTORED_SIZE,STATUS,CRC32

Your tasks

A. Extract only columns 1, 2 and 5 (ID, FILE_PATH and STATUS) from each CSV and save the stripped versions as  
   /home/user/backup_testing/daily_cols.txt and  
   /home/user/backup_testing/weekly_cols.txt.  
   • Keep the original header line.  
   • Preserve comma separation in the stripped files.

B. Combine the two stripped files *horizontally* so that the first row of the daily file is next to the first row of the weekly file, the second next to the second, and so on.  
   • Use the tab character (ASCII 0x09) as the delimiter between the two sides.  
   • Name the result /home/user/backup_testing/combined_restore_report.tsv.  
   • If one manifest has fewer rows than the other, the remaining cells on the shorter side must be left empty (paste’s normal behaviour).

C. Create a short plain-text summary, /home/user/backup_testing/final_summary.txt, that contains exactly three lines (no extra whitespace):
   Line 1: Total daily entries: <N>  
   Line 2: Total weekly entries: <M>  
   Line 3: Combined file created: combined_restore_report.tsv  
   where <N> and <M> are the number of *data* rows (do not count the header rows) in the daily and weekly manifests respectively.

D. While doing the work, record every shell command you run (one command per line, no prompts, no output, no comments) into  
   /home/user/backup_testing/task.log.  
   • The log must show the commands in the exact chronological order you executed them.  
   • Exactly those commands — nothing else — should appear (e.g. no stray editor launches).  
   • A minimum of five and a maximum of ten commands is acceptable.

Important format rules the automated checker will enforce
1. combined_restore_report.tsv must be a TAB-delimited text file.  
2. final_summary.txt must have exactly the three lines specified, with single spaces as shown, no trailing spaces.  
3. task.log must be a plain UTF-8 text file, one command per line, newline-terminated, between 5 and 10 lines inclusive.

Hints (you are free to ignore them)
• ‘cut -d, -f1,2,5’ is handy for slicing CSV columns.  
• ‘paste -d $\'\\t\' …’ can glue two files horizontally with a tab delimiter.  
• ‘wc -l’ can help you count lines.  
• Redirect (>>) to append each command into task.log right after you run it.

Make sure all required files end up with normal user-writable permissions (the defaults are fine). When everything is ready, double-check the three deliverables under /home/user/backup_testing.
truth:
Initial file contents (already present before the agent begins):

Path: /home/user/backup_testing/manifests/daily_restore_2023-01-15.csv
----------------------------------------------------------------------
ID,FILE_PATH,ORIGINAL_SIZE,RESTORED_SIZE,STATUS,CRC32
1,/data/db1.sql,1048576,1048576,OK,3A5F1D2C
2,/data/db2.sql,2048000,2048000,OK,B2C4E6F3
3,/data/db3.sql,1048576,1048572,WARNING,D3A7C9E4

Path: /home/user/backup_testing/manifests/weekly_restore_2023-01-15.csv
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
ID,FILE_PATH,ORIGINAL_SIZE,RESTORED_SIZE,STATUS,CRC32
1,/backup/www.tar.gz,4096000,4096000,OK,9C1D2E3F
2,/backup/mail.tar.gz,2097152,2097152,OK,AA11BB22
3,/backup/home.tar.gz,3145728,3145720,WARNING,CC33DD44
4,/backup/configs.tar.gz,512000,512000,OK,EE44FF55

Correct contents expected **after** successful task completion:

1. /home/user/backup_testing/daily_cols.txt
-------------------------------------------
ID,FILE_PATH,STATUS
1,/data/db1.sql,OK
2,/data/db2.sql,OK
3,/data/db3.sql,WARNING

2. /home/user/backup_testing/weekly_cols.txt
--------------------------------------------
ID,FILE_PATH,STATUS
1,/backup/www.tar.gz,OK
2,/backup/mail.tar.gz,OK
3,/backup/home.tar.gz,WARNING
4,/backup/configs.tar.gz,OK

3. /home/user/backup_testing/combined_restore_report.tsv
--------------------------------------------------------
ID,FILE_PATH,STATUS<TAB>ID,FILE_PATH,STATUS
1,/data/db1.sql,OK<TAB>1,/backup/www.tar.gz,OK
2,/data/db2.sql,OK<TAB>2,/backup/mail.tar.gz,OK
3,/data/db3.sql,WARNING<TAB>3,/backup/home.tar.gz,WARNING
<TAB>4,/backup/configs.tar.gz,OK

(Where <TAB> represents a single tab character 0x09.)

4. /home/user/backup_testing/final_summary.txt
----------------------------------------------
Total daily entries: 3
Total weekly entries: 4
Combined file created: combined_restore_report.tsv

5. /home/user/backup_testing/task.log
-------------------------------------
• Must contain between 5 and 10 non-empty lines.  
• Each line is a shell command the agent actually executed.  
• The specific commands may vary, so content is not hard-coded, but the test harness will:
  a) Assert line count is 5-10.  
  b) Verify every command line appears syntactically plausible (no empty lines).  
  c) Ensure the chronological order builds the requested files (timestamps not required).

File and directory permissions: default umask-derived (0644 for files, 0755 for dirs) are acceptable; no root ownership expected.

Environment assumptions:
• The agent launches with current working directory /home/user.  
• All paths given above are writeable by the agent’s normal user privileges.
